commands.review_summary:
Adds a public channel-broker SDK/plugin surface with official platform aliases, capability metadata, signed inbound/outbound broker handling, docs, tests, and package/labeler/test-shard updates.

PR surface: Source +2315, Tests +2392, Docs +373, Config +78, Generated 0, Other +14. Total +5172 across 50 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge blocker: source inspection shows the PR excludes `dist/extensions/channel-broker/**` while current packaged channel discovery and pack-artifact code rely on included `dist/extensions` package metadata. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can align the package files and pack-artifact test with the PR's bundled broker ownership.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `2f457ecc6f` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `2f457ecc6f` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening introduced in #86154 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest adversarial hardening and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` (targeted verification for the previously timed-out CI file) - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` signed-inbound proof: valid signed webhook returned 202 with dedupe key `acme:bot-main:telegram:evt-proof-1`; invalid signature returned 401 `invalid_signature`; oversized pre-auth body returned 413 `Payload too large`. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `2f457ecc6f` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `2f457ecc6f` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening introduced in #86154 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest adversarial hardening and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` (targeted verification for the previously timed-out CI file) - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` signed-inbound proof: valid signed webhook returned 202 with dedupe key `acme:bot-main:telegram:evt-proof-1`; invalid signature returned 401 `invalid_signature`; oversized pre-auth body returned 413 `Payload too large`. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.
